title: Uniface Memory Use
breadcrumbs: Development Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Uniface Memory Use Uniface runs on a wide range of platforms with different memory management characteristics, so Uniface includes its own memory management system. This enables Uniface to ignore the underlying memory management details of the operating system. However, this means that it is not always possible to optimize Uniface for a specific platform. The Uniface memory management software claims the memory needed for the Uniface program. It manages and reuses this memory throughout the life cycle of the program. Rather than explicitly releasing this memory, Uniface relies on the operating system to release all memory at the end of the program. As a result, common memory-checking tools often indicate a large number of false errors in Uniface. Conversely, common tools might not be able to identify all the genuine memory errors in Uniface. Furthermore, the memory usage of a running Uniface application can increase during the lifetime of the application, without necessarily indicating that the application has a real memory leak. The increasing demand on memory resources is explained by the following facts: Uniface loads data files (forms, services, reports) into a memory cache for performance reasons. When a new data file is read, the new file is stored in the cache, which is expanded if necessary. Uniface can require more memory to be allocated when processing a large data set for the first time. Uniface allocates memory for use by internal kernel data structures. The performance optimization of the kernel sometimes results in these data structures not being released for re-use after a given user action completes Some data structures remain resident in memory, so the same user action will be faster the second time. Related Links ProcScript and Memory When a component is compiled, any ProcScript is compiled into a tokenized intermediate format. This intermediate format code is then interpreted by Uniface at run time. The Uniface runtime places the compiled ProcScript into memory when the component is loaded, and then interprets it, one statement at a time. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
